projects.title: Surface Surveys
projects.narrative: Surface Surveys is a six-part photographic body of work made between 2015 and 2019 in remote and geologically significant terrains across four continents. The project studies how the Earth's surfaces record elemental force: sand, ice, lava, gypsum, granite, and parallel dune systems.
projects.location: Oman; Idaho; Greenland; New Mexico; Australia
projects.process: Aerial and ground-based photographic survey with medium-format Leica S007

descriptionSections.scientific: 2015-2019; six landscapes; four continents; medium-format Leica S007; aerial and ground-based surveys; three first artist-led aerial survey distinctions
descriptionSections.visual: elemental, abstract, evidentiary, mineral, glacial, volcanic, granular, wind-shaped, line-driven
descriptionSections.geographic: Greenland Ice Sheet; White Sands; Craters of the Moon; Simpson Desert; Wahiba Sands; City of Rocks
descriptionSections.atmospheric: remote, exposed, silent, glaring, wind-marked, cold, hot, immense
descriptionSections.spatial: aerial fields, close surfaces, vast terrain, compressed distance, serial landscape chapters

artworkAtoms.material: Archival pigment print on Canson Baryta Photographique II
artworkAtoms.dimensions: Variable by selected Surface Surveys artwork

projects.title: Surface Surveys: Greenland
projects.narrative: Greenland is the ice-sheet chapter of Surface Surveys. Within a project concerned with planetary surfaces shaped by elemental force, Greenland turns frozen water into surface, archive, terrain, and abstraction. It connects the survey's larger desert logic to glacial scale: meltwater, crevasse, sediment, blue pools, white fields, and the visible record of climate held in ice.
projects.process: Aerial photographic survey and field observation of ice, meltwater, crevasses, sediment, and glacial surface systems

descriptionSections.scientific: 2015 and 2017; Greenland Ice Sheet; largest ice sheet outside Antarctica; Ilulissat Icefjord context; first woman to complete a photographic aerial survey of the Greenland Ice Sheet in 2017
descriptionSections.visual: white, cobalt, turquoise, granular, fractured, pooled, veined, crystalline, sediment-speckled
descriptionSections.geographic: Greenland Ice Sheet; Ilulissat Icefjord; Arctic glacial terrain
descriptionSections.atmospheric: cold, remote, luminous, severe, quiet, suspended, immense
descriptionSections.spatial: aerial ice fields, crevasse networks, meltwater basins, broad white planes, blue channel systems

artworkAtoms.material: Archival pigment print on Canson Baryta Photographique II

projects.title: Surface Surveys: White Sands
projects.narrative: White Sands is the gypsum chapter of Surface Surveys. It brings the project into restricted airspace over the world's largest gypsum dunefield, where mineral brightness, military boundary, basin geology, and aerial access make white surface both landscape and threshold.
projects.location: White Sands National Park, New Mexico, USA
projects.process: Aerial photographic survey of gypsum dunes, restricted airspace, white mineral sand, basin systems, and glare

descriptionSections.scientific: 2018; world's largest gypsum dunefield; about 275 square miles; Tularosa Basin; restricted airspace; first artist-led photographic aerial survey of the gypsum dunefield
descriptionSections.visual: white, cool-toned, luminous, powdery, rippled, glaring, pale, wind-drawn
descriptionSections.geographic: White Sands National Park; Tularosa Basin; New Mexico; White Sands Missile Range and Holloman Air Force Base context
descriptionSections.atmospheric: bright, exposed, silent, reflective, austere, high-glare
descriptionSections.spatial: dune fields, basin floor, aerial distance, repeated ridges, open white expanse

artworkAtoms.material: Archival pigment print on Canson Baryta Photographique II
artworkAtoms.dimensions: Variable by selected White Sands artwork

projects.title: Surface Surveys: Wahiba
projects.narrative: Wahiba is the Oman sand-sea chapter of Surface Surveys. It places the larger project inside a living desert system where wind, monsoon cycles, Bedouin routes, sand color, and open night sky make surface both geological record and inhabited terrain.
projects.process: Ground-based desert field survey of wind-shaped sand, settlement routes, color, and surface movement

descriptionSections.scientific: 2017; Sharqiya / Wahiba Sands; about 180 km north-south by 80 km east-west; about 12,500 sq km; wind and monsoon-shaped sand sea
descriptionSections.visual: golden, rust-red, orange, rippled, granular, wind-combed, open, dry
descriptionSections.geographic: eastern Oman; Sharqiya Sands; Wahiba Sands; Bedouin villages and camel routes
descriptionSections.atmospheric: hot, quiet, exposed, expansive, nocturnal, sun-struck
descriptionSections.spatial: dune corridors, open sand fields, low horizon, shifting surface, long desert distance

artworkAtoms.material: Archival pigment print on Canson Baryta Photographique II

projects.title: Surface Surveys: Craters
projects.narrative: Craters is the volcanic Idaho chapter of Surface Surveys and the local anchor for The Observatory commission. It translates the basalt surface of Craters of the Moon into evidence, abstraction, darkness, and mineral memory, connecting the global survey back to Central Idaho's lava field and dark-sky terrain.
projects.location: Craters of the Moon National Monument, Idaho, USA
projects.process: Aerial and ground-based survey of basaltic lava field, cinder cones, spatter cones, fissures, sagebrush plains, and dark-sky terrain

descriptionSections.scientific: 2018; Craters of the Moon National Monument; basaltic lava field; aerial and ground-based study; first known artist-led photographic aerial survey of the monument
descriptionSections.visual: black, basaltic, fractured, matte, ridged, cooled, fissured, cratered, shadowed
descriptionSections.geographic: Central Idaho; Snake River Plain; Craters of the Moon National Monument; dark-sky landscape
descriptionSections.atmospheric: dark, volcanic, exposed, mineral, severe, ancient, lunar
descriptionSections.spatial: lava fields, pressure ridges, fissures, craters, black surface planes, panoramic wall scale

artworkAtoms.material: Archival pigment print on Canson Baryta Photographique II

projects.title: Surface Surveys: Simpson Desert
projects.narrative: Simpson Desert is the parallel-dune chapter of Surface Surveys. It expands the project into the scale of Australia's Munga-Thirri sand country, where long dune ridges, traditional Country, aerial distance, and red surface repetition become the structure of the work.
projects.process: Aerial photographic survey of long parallel dunes, red sand systems, desert scale, and dune-line repetition

descriptionSections.scientific: 2019; Simpson Desert / Munga-Thirri; about 176,500 sq km; parallel dunes; first known artist-led photographic aerial survey of the parallel dune field
descriptionSections.visual: red, linear, repetitive, wind-carved, immense, striated, dry, rhythmic
descriptionSections.geographic: South Australia; Queensland; Northern Territory; Munga-Thirri-Simpson Desert protected areas
descriptionSections.atmospheric: hot, remote, exposed, silent, expansive, ancient
descriptionSections.spatial: long dune ridges, parallel corridors, aerial bands, extended desert scale

artworkAtoms.material: Archival pigment print on Canson Baryta Photographique II
artworkAtoms.dimensions: Variable by selected Simpson Desert artwork

projects.title: Surface Surveys: City of Rocks
projects.narrative: City of Rocks is the granite chapter of Surface Surveys. It brings the body of work into a sculptural Idaho landscape where spires, fins, domes, monoliths, historic trail language, and exposed geologic time turn stone into vertical surface.
projects.location: City of Rocks National Reserve, Idaho, USA
projects.process: Ground-based granite field survey of spires, fins, domes, monoliths, sagebrush, and weathered stone surfaces

descriptionSections.scientific: 2017; City of Rocks National Reserve; protected 1988; Almo Pluton granite about 28 million years old; Green Creek Complex rocks over 2.5 billion years old
descriptionSections.visual: granite, vertical, weathered, pale, sculptural, fissured, sagebrush-framed
descriptionSections.geographic: southern Idaho; Albion Mountains; California Trail corridor; City of Rocks National Reserve
descriptionSections.atmospheric: dry, high-desert, quiet, historic, sunlit, resilient
descriptionSections.spatial: spires, fins, domes, monoliths, corridors, upright stone fields, sagebrush openings

artworkAtoms.material: Archival pigment print on Canson Baryta Photographique II
artworkAtoms.dimensions: Variable by selected City of Rocks artwork

projects.title: Omani Landscapes
projects.narrative: Omani Landscapes extends the studio's photographic surface practice into the arid mountain and desert terrain of Oman. The project uses color intervention and digital layering to heighten landform, heat, culture, and visual memory rather than simply document place.
projects.location: Jebel Akhdar and desert landscapes, Oman
projects.process: Digital layering and color intervention over Omani landscape photographs

descriptionSections.scientific: Oman archive sequence; Jebel Akhdar and desert landscape source folders; digital layering; color intervention over photographic landscape
descriptionSections.visual: pink, green, warm, saturated, arid, layered, graphic, mountain-shaped, dune-soft
descriptionSections.geographic: Jebel Akhdar; Oman; mountain and desert terrain
descriptionSections.atmospheric: warm, dry, vivid, spare, sunlit, heightened
descriptionSections.spatial: layered color fields, mountain faces, dune planes, constructed photographic surface

artworkAtoms.material: Archival pigment print on Canson Baryta Photographique II
artworkAtoms.dimensions: Variable by selected Omani Landscapes artwork
